concise_definition: Quick hitter 3-point offense : Swift actions that are generally designed to create scoring opportunities for a team’s best perimeter shooters, especially from three-point range.

sections.heading: What is quick hitter 3-point offense in basketball?
sections.text: Quick hitter 3-point offense in basketball comprises swift actions that are generally designed to create scoring opportunities for a team’s best perimeter shooters, especially from three-point range.

sections.heading: How to understand the player roles and diagrams on this page
sections.text:
In relation to any diagrams below, each of the offensive players are generally represented by the standardbasketball positionsin number format, unless otherwise noted.

In essence, 1 is thepoint guard; 2 is theshooting guard; 3 is thesmall forward; 4 is thepower forward; 5 is thecenter.

Furthermore, in terms of a brief legend for the diagrams:

- Black Dot or Small Circle:The black dot or small circle represents the basketball.

- Solid Arrow:The solid arrow indicates movement or a cut on the basketball court.

- Dotted Arrow:The dotted arrow represents a basketball pass.

- Wavy Arrow:The wavy arrow indicates dribbling.

- T-Shaped Symbol:The T-shaped symbol signifies a screen, also known as a pick.

- Double pipe symbol:The double pipe symbol denotes handoff action.

sections.heading: What is an example of quick hitter 3-point offense via a box set?
sections.text: This is an example of quick hitter 3-point offense via abox setthat includes adown screenas well as astagger screenandpick and popaction.

sections.heading: Part 1: Down screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring a down screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring a down screen.]

To begin, 2 cuts to the right side wing area via the down screen set by 4. Afterwards, 2 receives the ball from 1 who also cuts away to the vacant left side wing area.

It should be noted as well that 2 could take the three-point shot if that is open. Otherwise 2 simply keeps possession of the ball for the time being.

Also, as 2 makes their cut towards the wing, 3 cuts across the lane to the right side corner while 4 replaces at the left side low post area.

Following that, 5 pops to the top and receives the ball from 2. From there, 5reverses the ballwith 1 as the recipient of that pass.

sections.heading: Part 2: Stagger pick and pop
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring a stagger screen alongside pick and pop action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring a stagger screen alongside pick and pop action.]

Next, 4 lifts up to set a pick near the left side wing area. Furthermore, 1 utilizes that wing pick to dribble towards the high post area while 4 pops away to the left side corner.

At the same time, 3 cuts toward the extended right slot area via a stagger screen set by 2 and 5.

Afterwards, 1 could pass the ball to either 3 or 4, which could result in 3-point shot opportunities.

sections.heading: What is an example of quick hitter 3-point offense via a Horns set?
sections.text: This is an example of quick hitter 3-point offense via aHorns setthat features loop action as well as down screens andflare screens.

sections.heading: Part 1: Loop and wide pin down screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring loop action and a wide pin down screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring loop action and a wide pin down screen.]

To start, 1 executes adribble entrytowards the right side wing area. As that occurs, 2 performs azipper cuttowards the top via the down screen set by 4, replacing 1 in the process and completing the loop action.

Moreover, at the same time, on theweak side, 3 cuts to the left side wing via the wide pin down screen set by 5, who also cuts toward the left side low post area following the screening action.

From there, 2 could receive the ball from 1 and could possibly go ahead and take the 3-point shot if that is open.

Otherwise, 2 reverses it with 3 as the recipient, who could also take the 3-point shot if that is open. Otherwise, 3 simply retains possession of the ball.

sections.heading: Part 2: Double flare
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring double flare screens.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of quick hitter 3-point offense featuring double flare screens.]

Next, 1 and 4 cut towards the high post area to set double flare screens. 2 uses those screens by cutting away towards the right side wing area.

3 can throw a skip pass, which could be received by 2, resulting in another potential 3-point shot opportunity.
